description: When a hot-patch file download is triggered using the switch WebUI, log messages can incorrectly state that the file is added to the database with a missing status. This is a temporary state, and will correctly change to Not applied once the download is completed.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.0010, what caveat is documented for Hot Patch?

Feature Caveat: Hot Patch - When a hot-patch file download is triggered using the switch WebUI, log messages can incorrectly state that the file is added to the database with a missing status. This is a temporary state, and will correctly change to Not applied once the download is completed.

description: Only static VXLAN is supported.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.0010, what caveat is documented for VXLAN?
Feature Caveat: VXLAN - Only static VXLAN is supported.

description: The R8V13A 6200F Switch does not support mixed RF stacking.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.0010, what caveat is documented for VSF?
Feature Caveat: VSF - The R8V13A 6200F Switch does not support mixed RF stacking.

description: When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. If a local or external SNMP MIB walk is performed in the interval between when SNMP is first enabled and the SNMP daemon is ready, the MIB walk action will return an error.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.0010, what caveat is documented for SNMP?

Feature Caveat: SNMP - When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. If a local or external SNMP MIB walk is performed in the interval between when SNMP is first enabled and the SNMP daemon is ready, the MIB walk action will return an error.

description: When a switch uses a certificate with a legacy certificate name that is not supported in 10.12 because it contains disallowed characters, the information will migrate properly in the upgrade, but that certificate can no longer be edited. For new certificate names, only alphanumeric characters, dots, dashes, and underscores are allowed.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.0010, what caveat is documented for Certificates?

Feature Caveat: Certificates - When a switch uses a certificate with a legacy certificate name that is not supported in 10.12 because it contains disallowed characters, the information will migrate properly in the upgrade, but that certificate can no longer be edited. For new certificate names, only alphanumeric characters, dots, dashes, and underscores are allowed.

description: In a VSF stack, the switch may fail to log events for the matching access-list entries. ACL functionality is not impacted; access-list entries are applied properly and only the logging is incorrectly generated.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.0010, what caveat is documented for ACLs?

Feature Caveat: ACLs - In a VSF stack, the switch may fail to log events for the matching access-list entries. ACL functionality is not impacted; access-list entries are applied properly and only the logging is incorrectly generated.

description: VSF stack formation is blocked when there are reserved autojoin interfaces (25, 26, 49, 50) in the stack topology.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.0010, what caveat is documented for Aruba CX Mobile App?
Feature Caveat: Aruba CX Mobile App - VSF stack formation is blocked when there are reserved autojoin interfaces (25, 26, 49, 50) in the stack topology.

description: For Classifier policy modifications to be secure, Aruba strongly encourages modifications be done as a three-step process: Bring down the port, modify, and bring the port back up.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.0010, what caveat is documented for Classifiers?

Feature Caveat: Classifiers - For Classifier policy modifications to be secure, Aruba strongly encourages modifications be done as a three-step process: Bring down the port, modify, and bring the port back up.

description: Policies containing both MAC and IPv6 classes are not allowed.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.0010, what limitation is mentioned for Classifiers?
Feature Caveat: Classifiers - Policies containing both MAC and IPv6 classes are not allowed.

description: No other checkpoint besides "startup-configuration" gets migrated during the upgrade process.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.0010, what caveat is documented for CMF?
Feature Caveat: CMF - No other checkpoint besides "startup-configuration" gets migrated during the upgrade process.

description: The switch may only software forward at a rate of 100pps if the packets that trigger ICMP redirect.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.0010, what caveat is documented for ICMP Redirect?
Feature Caveat: ICMP Redirect - The switch may only software forward at a rate of 100pps if the packets that trigger ICMP redirect.

description: MACsec works between a CX device and a Windows VM running AnyConnect with AES-128 cipher. AnyConnect does not support AES-256 in the NAM module (works only for the VPN module).

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.0010, what caveat is documented for MACsec?

Feature Caveat: MACsec - MACsec works between a CX device and a Windows VM running AnyConnect with AES-128 cipher. AnyConnect does not support AES-256 in the NAM module (works only for the VPN module).

description: When Cisco AnyConnect is used as dot1x supplicant, it is recommended to configure cak-length to be 16 under dot1x-authenticator mode.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.0010, what limitation is mentioned for MACsec?
Feature Caveat: MACsec - When Cisco AnyConnect is used as dot1x supplicant, it is recommended to configure cak-length to be 16 under dot1x-authenticator mode.

description: Ensure the cipher suite GCM-AES-128 is configured when AOS-CX is acting as a key server. This is because, by default AOS-CX will use the most secure cipher suite gcm-aes-xpn-256 for establishing MACsec secure link and Comware/PVOS doesn’t support an XPN cipher suite.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.0010, what additional caveat is documented for MACsec?

Feature Caveat: MACsec - Ensure the cipher suite GCM-AES-128 is configured when AOS-CX is acting as a key server. This is because, by default AOS-CX will use the most secure cipher suite gcm-aes-xpn-256 for establishing MACsec secure link and Comware/PVOS doesn’t support an XPN cipher suite.

description: OSPFv2 and OSPFv3 do not support detailed LSA show commands.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.0010, what caveat is documented for OSPF?
Feature Caveat: OSPF - OSPFv2 and OSPFv3 do not support detailed LSA show commands.

description: Authorization by means of HPE VSAs is not supported.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.0010, what caveat is documented for RADIUS?
Feature Caveat: RADIUS - Authorization by means of HPE VSAs is not supported.

description: REST supports the 'admin' and 'operator' roles but does not work with TACACS+ command authorization.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.0010, what caveat is documented for REST?
Feature Caveat: REST - REST supports the 'admin' and 'operator' roles but does not work with TACACS+ command authorization.

description: RIP/RIPng metric configuration support is not available.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.0010, what caveat is documented for RIP/RIPng?
Feature Caveat: RIP/RIPng - RIP/RIPng metric configuration support is not available.

scenario: This issue only impact switches onboarded to Aruba Central

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.0010, what is the workaround for Internal srvcs: Yang Bug 291567?
Internal srvcs: Yang (Bug ID 291567): No workaround is documented in the release notes.

description: Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

description: Symptom: On a dual port AP, a device profile client failed to onboard on a LAG interface with secure mode enabled. This issue is seen on a device profile with lldp-group matching criteria, but is not observed on a device profile with mac-match matching criteria. Scenrio: This issue occurs when a user connects the Dual AP on some normal physical ports (for example, 1/1/1 and 1/1/2) and makes sure these two ports NOT part of the LAG. LLDP frames exchanged and the output of the show lldp neighbors command shows the entries. Next, configure LAG with AAA and port-access commands to enable security. Add these twp ports to the LAG interface and then observe the Dual AP onboard failure by issuing the command show port-access clients interface lag<lag id>. Workaround: Clear LLDPneighbors and toggle the LAG interface on DUT.

description: Symptom: Traffic convergence takes approximately two minutes when VSF switchover is performed. Scenario: This issue occurs when traffic is flowing through the switch using the uplink on the conductor. Performing a VSF switchover causes the standby to become the new conductor, and it takes approximately 2 minutes for traffic to resume using the uplink of the new conductor. Workaround: If the Uplink from the VSF is a LAG with members in Conductor/Standby/Member, the convergence time would be lesser and around 70 seconds.

L3 Routes (Bug ID 207077): The documented workaround is: If the Uplink from the VSF is a LAG with members in Conductor/Standby/Member, the convergence time would be lesser and around 70 seconds.

section: Manual Configuration Restore for Software Downgrade
task: Manual Configuration Restore for Software Downgrade
procedure: Use the show checkpoint command to see the saved checkpoints and ensure that you have a checkpoint that is an exact match of the target software version (see the Image Version column in the output of the command, for example, ML.10.xx.yyyy).
Copy the backup checkpoint into the startup-config.
Boot the switch to the target version (lower version), making sure to select no when prompted to save the current configuration.
description: Use the show checkpoint command to see the saved checkpoints and ensure that you have a checkpoint that is an exact match of the target software version (see the Image Version column in the output of the command, for example, ML.10.xx.yyyy). Copy the backup checkpoint into the startup-config. Boot the switch to the target version (lower version), making sure to select no when prompted to save the current configuration.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.0010, how do you restore configuration after a software downgrade?

Downgrade Restore: To restore configuration after a software downgrade, follow the documented procedure in order: 1. Use the show checkpoint command to see the saved checkpoints and ensure that you have a checkpoint that is an exact match of the target software version (see the Image Version column in the output of the command, for example, ML.10.xx.yyyy). 2. Copy the backup checkpoint into the startup-config. 3. Boot the switch to the target version (lower version), making sure to select no when prompted to save the current configuration.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.0010, what is the manual configuration restore procedure for software downgrade?

Downgrade Restore: To restore configuration after a software downgrade, follow the documented procedure in order: 1. Use the show checkpoint command to see the saved checkpoints and ensure that you have a checkpoint that is an exact match of the target software version (see the Image Version column in the output of the command, for example, ML.10.xx.yyyy). 2. Copy the backup checkpoint into the startup-config. 3. Boot the switch to the target version (lower version), making sure to select no when prompted to save the current configuration.

procedure: Copy the new image into the non-current boot bank on the switch using your preferred method.

Depending on the version being updated, there may be device component updates needed. Preview any devices updates needed using the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command and entering n when asked to continue.

When ready to update the system, if a non-failsafe device update is needed, make sure the system will not have any power interruption during the process. Invoke the allow unsafe updates command to allow updates to proceed after a switch reboot. Proceed to step 4 within the configured time.
Use the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command to initiate the upgrade. On the switch console port, output similar to the documented example will be displayed as various components are being updated.

When ready to update the system, if a non-failsafe device update is needed, make sure the system will not have any power interruption during the process. Invoke the allow unsafe updates command to allow updates to proceed after a switch reboot. Proceed to step 4 within the configured time. messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.0010, how do you perform the software upgrade?

Upgrade Procedure: To perform the software upgrade, follow the documented procedure in order: 1. Copy the new image into the non-current boot bank on the switch using your preferred method. 2. Depending on the version being updated, there may be device component updates needed. Preview any devices updates needed using the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command and entering n when asked to continue. 3. When ready to update the system, if a non-failsafe device update is needed, make sure the system will not have any power interruption during the process. Invoke the allow unsafe updates command to allow updates to proceed after a switch reboot. Proceed to step 4 within the configured time. 4. Use the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command to initiate the upgrade. On the switch console port, output similar to the documented example will be displayed as various components are being updated.
